title: 25 Essential Thought Leaders in Semiconductors
gist: A thought-leader roundup framed around 2025-2026 industry economics: ~1.05 trillion chips sold in 2025 at $0.74 average price, ~$19B to build an advanced fab, ~$1B to design a chip, and a KPMG confidence index at 63 where tariffs/trade policy displaced talent as leaders' top concern for the first time.

insights.claim: Approximately 1.05 trillion chips were sold globally in 2025 at an average price of about $0.74 per chip, per Deloitte's 2026 outlook.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: The trillion-unit, sub-dollar-average baseline anchors the gap between commodity volume and high-value AI silicon where profit concentrates.
insights.quote: Approximately 1.05 trillion chips were sold globally in 2025 at an average price of US$0.74 per chip
insights.locator: Why Semiconductor Thought Leadership Matters Right Now

insights.claim: Building a single advanced fabrication facility now costs approximately $19 billion, and designing each new chip costs approximately $1 billion on average.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: $19B fab and $1B design costs explain industry consolidation and why only a few players can compete at the leading edge.
insights.quote: Building a single advanced fabrication facility now costs approximately US$19 billion, and designing each new chip costs approximately US$1 billion on average.
insights.locator: Why Semiconductor Thought Leadership Matters Right Now

insights.claim: The KPMG 2026 Global Semiconductor Industry Confidence Index reached 63, its third-highest reading in two decades, driven by AI demand.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Near-record confidence signals sustained AI-driven capex; a supportive macro backdrop for semi equipment and foundry names.
insights.quote: The KPMG 2026 Global Semiconductor Industry Confidence Index reached 63, the third highest in two decades, driven by AI demand.

insights.claim: For the first time in the KPMG survey's history, industry leaders rank tariffs and trade policy as their top concern, above talent shortages and competition.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Trade policy now outranks talent as the dominant industry risk—reinforces onshoring deals and geopolitical hedging as investment themes.
insights.quote: for the first time in the survey's history, industry leaders now rank tariffs and trade policy as their top concern, above talent shortages and competition

insights.claim: The article frames the semiconductor industry as simultaneously a technology, geopolitics, supply-chain, sustainability and workforce story, arguing decisions on who makes chips, where and for whom will shape competitive advantage and national security for decades.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: A multi-dimensional lens for evaluating semi investments beyond pure technology; policy and supply-chain factors are now first-order.
insights.locator: The semiconductor industry is not just another sector

insights.claim: SemiWiki founder Daniel Nenni has over four decades in the global semiconductor industry including senior roles at Intel, co-authored 'Fabless' and 'Mobile Unleashed', and his LinkedIn network has reached the platform's 30,000-connection limit.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Context on the credibility and reach of SemiWiki as a practitioner signal source worth monitoring for early industry intelligence.
insights.quote: His LinkedIn network has reached the platform's 30,000 connection limit
